review_text: Beautiful place to spend time with the whole family. We stayed in a cabin that had everything we needed. Staff were friendly and helpful. Will be coming back for the third time soon!

Lovely staff, lovely campers, gorgeous park in Autumn I was blown away. Speaking of blown away, every morning at 7am the symphony of 3 petrol leaf blowers and a truck out on the street metres away. Jesus Christ is there a worse invention? Can we not pay twice as many people with rakes?

Okay so I presume the park themselves don't employ these people, but if i'd have known I never would have come here. Not to sound so spoiled but it's a HOLIDAY park, you dont expect that level of a disturbance. Obviously, only a problem in the peak of Autumn.

The amenities were visibly maintained, no complaints. Couple of showers with heads on the way out, couple of kitchen taps that don't work and our plug for the van wouldn't hold the head of our cord due to being slightly broken. 3 of 4 washing machines out of order. Not a problem as it wasn't busy, but all added up to add a slight impression of decay. Very slight I want to be clear.

Location to town is excellent but those leaf blowers.. that's all I'll remember. I don't blame the park, but if you guys can give a heads up or even better have a chat with whoever runs them about maybe doing it later in the day that'd be best.
I love visiting Bright this time of year. Change of season brings a different outlook.
Good friendly place.
Very disappointed to see caravan owners running their grey water directly onto the ground.  NRMA need to be a lot stricter about this rule.

Stayed during the 2026 Bright Autumn Festival and location of park made going to the various activities easy. Booked an ensuite site which are located away from the general camping areas and therefore quieter. Access to site was good and depending on ensuite site allocated, site size is also good. All sites appear to be slightly sloped down toward the Ovens River.
Fantastic park. Very clean. Superb location. Friendly staff. Grounds very picturesque. Just wanted to stay longer! Highly recommend this park.
Lovely place and staff!

We had such a lovely three-night stay at NRMA Bright. We booked an unpowered waterside site and it couldn’t have been better. Peaceful, scenic, and a perfect base for a few slow days away.

The park itself is incredibly well kept, with everything clean, tidy, and thoughtfully laid out. The open fires in the evenings were a standout (there’s nothing quite like that cosy glow after a day outdoors), and being able to bring the dog along made it even more special for us.

A real highlight, especially as the evenings cooled down, were the heated bathrooms. Such a simple touch, but it made a big difference and didn’t go unnoticed.

We’d happily return and wouldn’t hesitate to recommend it to anyone looking for a relaxed, family-friendly stay in Bright.
If your visiting Bright this is a great location only a few minutes walk into the town centre.  The park itself is very spacious with lots of amenities and very clean and tidy
Great caravan park, heaps for the kids to do, we were really happy with the amenities.
Love this place, its so beautiful with all the trees, creek and fires burning around the camp sites.

We have been coming to this caravan park every Easter for the past 40 years. We’ve always come back because we love the proximity of the park and the sites are generous.|However the past few years, and particularly this past Easter we feel that the Park is at a low point in accomodation standards. We have a caravan but the sites  were uneven, dirty,dusty. There was a huge pile of dirt next to our site that has been there for months. There was also a log surrounded by orange tape between our site and the creek so made it very hazardous to walk around. But our main concerns are the toilets and laundry . They were dirty and we never saw a cleaner there for the whole Easter week. If they were there they didn’t clean. Bins overflowing in the ladies, shower outlets blocked , benches filthy. We constantly get told this toilet block (closest to the township) is only used in peak periods. It has not been updated in 40 years. So even if it is closed alotof the time it should be up to standard when it is open. Putting up a sign saying “we’re sorry about the condition of toilets but only used in peak periods” is totally unacceptable . We’re still being charging peak rates for sub standard amenities.|The laundry has 5 washing machines but only 1 works. Very inadequate for 2 years running|I think it’s time some money was spent to upgrade facilities or we’ll be looking elsewhere: we book 3 to 4 sites every Easter

We have stayed here twice now and would highly recommend. It is so close to town so you can walk everytime. It has a pool and spa and a great playground too. We stay in a two bedroom feathertop villa and it is wonderful. The best part is it has a BBQ on the verandah so we can cook our breakfast and dinner out there. However it would be great to have more things to use when cleaning it. The room is neat and tidy however the couch could be upgraded as it is quite stained. We also had mould in our bathroom on the roof. Staff are always so kind and helpful. We would highly recommend staying here and we can't wait to come back again.

Great location - right next to the brewery which was great. We had an unpowered spot right next to the river which was lovely. It’s a very busy campsite during busy periods, so do bare that in mind, but considering how busy it was, the facilities were immaculate.

Our third straight year of choosing NRMA for our family holiday and each year I find it harder and harder to leave. This park has to be one of the most beautiful parks I have ever been to. With the trees surrounding you, the ducks, the birds and the river running through, you feel like you’re lost in a magical forest. The facilities are fantastic, the staff are lovely and truly can’t wait to come back. 2027 is already booked and I’m counting down the days. Thank you for the memories NRMA Bright, can’t wait to make more!

I booked over the ph for 1 night paying $62 for 1 night. On arrival I drove past lovely clean sites with grass. Mine was full dirt. The closest amenities highlighted to me by the lady on arrival were shockingly derelict and filthy. The toilet bowl was stained paint had peeled off the walls. I reported my concerns and was told I could be moved but I was hooked up and set up. I walked further up to other much nicer amenities.
Very disappointed with the response I got the next day from the manager.
Didn't book another night with NRMA but booked 2 nights at Mt Buffalo caravan Park  for $40 a night

Park needs an upgrade. It's is very large and has spacious sites under a canopy of trees to the rear of the park. Other areas were very dry and dusty, Clearly the whole region is in drought, but surely some effort can be made to make the park more appealing and tidy.| Some amenities are dated, open to the weather. More modern amenities had leaking and mouldy showers. Laundry near our site had a layer of dust over the benches, windows that hadn't been cleaned for some time and 5 out of 6 washing machines not working.|Clearly  NRMA needs to spend some of their million dollar profits on maintaining their assets.
Big park running along the stream - we rang up to book with a couple of site preferences and were given a lovely spot that completely met the brief.  Friendly staff and good facilities

This park is in a fantastic location and we have been going there for years but the toilet block and camp kitchen room in front of the office needs attention. After visiting other parks near by I'm needing to write this. Can the management please go to the ladies and check out the vents above showers, mould on the tiles, has the A/C ever been serviced. I asked one of the staff if they would put a maintenance report for a screw on the shower scrapper so it does not fall apart when trying to clean the shower floor but nothing happened. We often use the camp kitchen on a very hot day for relief and enjoy watching the tennis but no one could get free TV on the television.  Instead we sat and listened to the game machines that I'm sure parents do not want in the park. We did notice this year how friendly the staff were compared to other years this was appreciated. Children's bath room needs attention also. Only one washing machine in that block is also not good enough at peak times. I hope this helps to further the improvement of the park for the long term visitors and new visitors.

IF YOU ARE CAMPING IN TENTS THEN ITS NOT FOR YOU,  loud noise from road , loud music noise , showers dirty  toilets dirty,  definitely not , you can hire a hotel room for
Similar price. If you love to waking up whole night, then go for it

Been coming here for years, and it is in a great location with beautiful trees and river outlook.
Unfortunately maintenance has not been prioritised. Most toilets in our block are missing their flush  buttons, only one soap dispenser works. Easy fixes, but these issues are not attended to. TV in the camp kitchen has disappeared this year which is disappointing as we love to watch the AO there. All the sites are virtually devoid of grass which means the bathrooms get very muddy. A small amount of effort is all that is required to fix these issues, but they persist year after year.

We camped here for two nights (Fri, Sat) in mid-January 2026, booking at late notice.  We were in Bright for an Audax road cycling event.
The park is in a great location, literally a few hundred metres from the main shopping centre of Bright, with easy access via walking paths.
The express check-in was good, but the boom gate access did not operate successfully for some minutes. I also found it difficult to find my unpowered site (the site numbers were virtually non-existent), and only with looking closely at the map was I able to deduce which site we had booked.
The unpowered site itself (171) was basically dirt and very dusty, enough to *just* fit my 6.5m long campervan and a tent. It was under a large oak tree which was nice for shade, in front of an amenity block. The toilet block and shower block (separated) were very average and not particularly clean (faeces smeared on the toilet lid for a number of days!). There were no shower curtains and so the water goes everywhere in the cubicle. The laundry in the amenities block (2 x troughs) was basically used to wash dishes and charge devices.
The camp sites around us appeared to be very small and tight with very little space between sites. The riverside ones were the smallest from what I could see.
The river running through the campground seemed to attract the most attention (it was mid-summer), with people using the cool running water to sit in, lie in or play in. There were plenty of young families with many bikes being ridden through the camp, as well as quite a few dogs being walked. The Mystic MTB Park is a kilometer or so from the caravan park, and there seemed to be quite a few people riding to/from the park.
People were respectfully quiet at night after 9pm. There was a coffee cart in the park which seemed to be super busy in the morning. I didn't use the pool, but my cycling partner did and said it was good.
Overall, I would say that the park is *just* satisfactory but I wouldn't go back there again. There are better options and compared to many other campgrounds I have stayed in, it just wasn't up to scratch. The sites could be more appealing (grassed) and the amenity block upgraded for a start.

Traveling for three months across multiple states and this is by far the worst experience we have had. Pool closed due to parts that need replacing, multiple laundry locations closed or out of order and dirty, site flooded four times in one day and manager couldn’t be contacted by staff. Everything you use is in need of repair or breaks. The only thing the park has going for it is the beautiful river and lovely bike path into town for kids.

We stayed on a camp site, so no comment on the rooms/cabins.
Staff were friendly and helpful. Facilities were cleaned frequently. The park is walking distance from the main street and the water park/swimming hole.
Great spot

We stayed in a powered site with our little dog. The facilities were very clean with heaps of hot water. We all loved the river running through the area and it was an easy walk to town. Very dog friendly town. Friendly helpful staff.

Great park very spacious around the grounds , We stayed in the ensuite section very cool under the trees peaceful lots birds around. Ensuite was great ,great showers also highly recommended to stay in this park walking distance to town approximately 2 minutes walk

I booked a powered site for a tent. The allocated site did not have a single blade of grass. The whole site was covered with sharp gravel and the wind was blowing dust everywhere. I ended up abandoning that and pitching on a grassy section adjacent my friend's caravan site otherwise the floor of my tent would have been shredded.
Facilities were clean and the location is great right near the shops and the river.
Great spot for camping

En mycket vacker park med Ovens river som rinner genom, nära till stan ändå lugn och fridfull.
Men flera av toaletter och dusch fungerade inte som ska och var inte så rena heller.
I köket värme kaminen och tv fungerade inte. Det fanns ingen tvål eller kökshanddukar där.
Det är synd för en sådan vacker park.
Been coming here for about 20years. It really is one of my favourite places. It's beautiful in every season.

We rocked up to this park 5mins before the office closed, just by luck, as we were booked in at Tasman park Bright, but the spot they allocated to us was too small for our caravan, so we were on a mission to find another caravan park in the area that would accomadate our caravan.

After checking for any availability for that same night, I was told there was no availability, which was quite understandable, being that it was the melbourne cup long weekend.  So I thanked them and just mentioned quickly the situation we were in and walked out.

Kids were disappointed, and so was I and my husband to be honest, because this park looked amazing! But to our surprise one of the guys ran out of the office after us and said the manager might be able to do something for you guys!

He offered us a spot in the long term accomadation area, and we were soooo happy!

THIS PARK IS ABSOLUTELY AMAZING!

it doesn't have a fancy pool, and fancy playgrounds BUT it has a beautiful river running thru the caravan park, ovens river, which is the perfect place to play, chill and swim in.

The staff are all super friendly and always willing to help.

So if you are looking for a cabin or a caravan park in Bright, this is the place to go!

What a fantastic stay at NRMA Bright Caravan Park! Ashleigh gave us such a warm and friendly welcome & recommendations of places of interest. The park itself is leafy beautiful, with a lovely creek running right through it, perfect for relaxing. Camp fires are allowed & the ambiance this creates adds to the magic of the stay. Bright is such a fantastic town – great cafes, stunning scenery, and plenty to do. Highly recommend this spot for anyone visiting the area!

What a gem! Ashleigh greeted us with such a warm smile and awesome tips on what to see and do around town. The park grounds are absolutely gorgeous — leafy, peaceful, and with a lovely creek running right through it (the perfect chill-out spot!). The amenities are clean and we particularly appreciate the heated bathrooms! We loved that campfires are allowed — nothing beats sitting around the fire under the stars, soaking up that mountain magic. Bright itself is just brilliant — great cafés, many breweries, stunning scenery, and loads to explore. Highly recommend this park if you’re heading this way… we’ll definitely be back!

NRMA Bright is marketed as dog-friendly park, which we appreciated, but there isn’t an off-leash area. We attempted to throw a ball for our dogs and ended up in trouble due to leash rules, which was disappointing. The park is clean and pleasant for a leash-only visit, but the strict enforcement around dogs, limits what you can do, especially for active dogs who want to run and fetch. If you need a place where dogs can roam off-leash, this isn’t it. A bit more flexibility would be appreciated ..
we stayed in a powered site. facilities were very clean and modern. bathrooms heated. loved the river running through it. easy walk to town across bridge. friendly helpful staff
It was a great place but the weather was a bit cold

Could not fault this park, the hosts were amazing, cleanliness of facilities were quite good and great location with everything in walking distance.
Thankyou for allowing pets, Murphy had a great time🐶
We will be returning ✨️
You can pay $5 to use dump point 👍 Very Handy thank you.
Nice place for staying. Close to shops and restaurants. Perfect value for money .
Stayed in a powered site for 7 nights. Reasonably priced but tiny sites with nowhere to park my car. No laundry facilities. Clean amenities. Walking distance to all the bars and shops in town
It’s always a pleasure coming here every year.

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Bright holiday park . We had our RV set up right next to the creek which was so peaceful to listen to . Our fur babies had plenty of room to roam around and enjoy the space . The bathroom facilities were not working unfortunately, so we did have to rely on our Rv for showers / toilet etc .. not a huge deal but always nice to have a bigger space for a shower. Everything else was fantastic, the service was exceptional and amount of pet friendly places to eat were great . Highly recommend and will be back .

Powered site . The owners were fantastic from greeting to helping us with advice on our misbehaving awning . 2 minute walk into town. Rivers flows through the caravan park adorned with trees . Clean and heated amenities with excellent water pressure . Will be back !
Beautiful park good location very friendly staff.

We stayed here recently 23 May to 25th. We loved every minute of it , Josh and family were great , there was a bit of a hiccup with our booking but it was sorted very quickly.  We stayed in cabin 2 , overlooking the small creek , we had a fire on Saturday night , as did a few cabins and sights. The park is quiet and the sound of the creek day and night is very serene. We are already planning our next holiday there.
Thank you Josh and family.

We recently stayed, intially on an ensuite site and then, after our camper was damaged in a collision, in a cabin.  The managers, Ashlee and Josh, were extremely helpful, going above and beyond to provide a cabin and assistance. The ensuite sites are excellent, clean and well appointed, as are the cabins. The location in the centre of town is excellent.
Nice but tight sites for caravan.
Beautiful park and very good amenities
Worst caravan park ever, no idea on what’s going on, showers were cold and not clean at all. Disorganised and poorly managed. Never come here

I've stayed a number of times and keep coming  back because I find the cost reasonable for a camp site, the creek is gorgeous, its super close to town with restaurants literally across the road, lots of amenities and access to the mtb trail head is easy via a cute little track along the creek upstream.

The only thing that dampened the latest trip was the geezer in the park who played the bagpipes for an hour or two every afternoon. Unbearable.

Loved this Caravan Park! Stayed for 4 nights on a powered site. Amenities were great, location superb as we could easily walk into town with beautiful surrounding areas by the river. So much to do, with amazing places to see. Love the Victorian Alps! This place is also a haven for cyclists, so take you bicycles with you or rent them here if you like. Pet friendly as well, plenty of shade as it is set amongst gorgeous trees. We will be back for sure!
Lovely place, good team, sorted out past niggles in the booking process.

I've been camping here for around 15 years, its an amazing camping location for people with tents, caravans and those who would prefer a cabin! The scenery is lovely and its so close to the centre of the town making activities super easy to do! Love it here!

Awesome caravan park, they allow dogs and we had the best time. Really close to town and lovely atmosphere. People are respectful at night so you can rest assure you’ll get a good sleep. Location is great and if you are there with a good crew then you’ll have a great time. 👍👌

Disappointed. Were told we had a great site and easy access. Ended up having to look at 5 sites before we found one that fit our van (only 8m). Promised the earth and delivered a hot and sweaty experience having tried to access multiple sites before finding one that fit.

Care factor zero, nice park however they double booked our site and seemed to care less, we had booked 3 months in advance, we were then stuck on a tiny site at the back of the park next to the smelly bins with no where to park our car , we had previously provided and they had confirmed the measurements of the van, there were many sites that seemed adequate but the staff were unhelpful

Enjoyed our visit to Bright NRMA caravan park.
The site we were allocated was very tight when parking the caravan especially with caravans located in front and at the side.
The Van site was built up with road base and the site sloped about 4 degrees towards the road this caused the caravan hitch to bottom out on the site making it difficult to get the van in position.
So some sites need to be looked at and levelled out.
Not sure how often the toilet blocks were cleaned, thought they could have been looked after a little better from a hygiene point of view.
On a he whole enjoyed the stay.

Lovely location but disappointed with the cleanliness of the amenities block.
Stayed 5 nights and never saw cleaners the whole time.  The cleaners log chart in amenities,  showed the last date cleaned as some 2 weeks earlier.
We stayed in our RV. The site next to the playground was large but not level, and our choks weren't enough to level the van.

Stayed ensuite site 327. Avoid at ll costs unless you like acorns dropping on your van all night and day. A site that is truly not worth paying $1. Not level, difficult to get into, amenities  for plugging in distant and wrong side of van. A total waste of money when there are many caravan parks in the area that are better. Won't be returning to this park again.

Lovely site and location. I needed a ladder to fix some trim on top of our van. They came with a ladder and actually did the repair job for me. Excellent service above and beyond the call of duty. Thanks so much. Visited a few times now and will certainly be back.
Wonderful place. Lots of shade. If you want to use Starlink you will have trouble.
Lovely park. Amenities very clean. Staff need to know size of sites. Saw too many vans trying to get into small areas. Very tight spaces for reversing.

Stopped there without a booking on a Wednesday afternoon. The staff were fantastic. Let us park out van inside and have a wander around to see if we liked it. Have us a map marked with available sites. Stayed the night and was told there was a special price as it was off peak! The pool and amenities were spot on. Great vibe. We will return!  And pet friendly.
Advised the park was not dog friendly until after 28th January so we booked a much more expensive option. After walking through the park today 24th and seeing 5 dogs on sites very disappointed.

The location of this campground is fantastic just a stone’s throw from the town centre with plenty of great facilities nearby. The river frontage adds to the charm, and the overall setting is quite scenic and peaceful.

The area we stayed in was sloped and lacked grass, which made it less comfortable for setting up, but this could be improved by adding more grassy spaces and a bit more care for the grounds, especially during the busy season. The toilets were clean during the day, though they did get a bit dirty at night. A solution like mats or foot cleaners could help keep them tidier.

Despite these minor issues, I would absolutely recommend this caravan park to others. The proximity to the city, coupled with the facilities and beautiful setting, makes it a great option for anyone looking for a convenient and relaxing place to stay.

This park requires immediate attention to address ongoing maintenance issues.  Deteriorating facilities, including broken washing machines and unsanitary restrooms, negatively impact the visitor experience.  These persistent problems necessitate prompt action from management.  Allowing these conditions to persist reflects poorly on the park's reputation and discourages long-term patronage.  Investing in necessary repairs and improvements will demonstrate a commitment to guest satisfaction and preserve the park's appeal.

Lovely place to stay for the night. As we are staying in the camping area it is approx 300 meters to the bathrooms (be aware if you really need to go!). As it's school holidays, warm and quite busy many people "claim" their spot at the the river entries making coming and going a bit difficult. Camp sites are mostly level, a bit bumpy and a few rocks in the ground that the tent pegs weren't happy about. Only place that we could get drinking water was from the camp kitchen - no taps close to the site.
Well done to the staff who organised late check in because I didn't arrive as early as expected and for putting us as far away as possible from other campers (knowing campers don't like being squished together).
Kitchens and toilets were nice and clean.
Great to see kids if all ages, welcome everyone from all over the site to come together for a game if cricket.
Due to the availability of water, toilets and river, the camp is a bit over priced. Would like to come back but will probably stay in a cabin.

Despite being within walking distance to the centre of Bright, this is a bit of a run down park.  Amenities whilst clean, could do with a makeover.  The sites 218 thru to 227 are under a heavy tree canopy with zero grass.  These sites are that sloped, the draw bar of the Vans were resting on the ground.  We had site #445.  Dusty, no grass and tight to back in.  Whilst the trees are nice to look at, less trees will encourage the grass to grow.

The next time thru Bright, it will be a different Park.
We’ve stayed several times and love the location. Only issue was our site sloped significantly so we couldn’t get the van level.

Its a beautiful large park winding along the banks of a river in town, and very close to the center for that matter,  and all the push bike tracks and main rail trail, oh and its very dog friendly..
The majestic tree canopy is stunning overhead which offers shade and tranquillity to relax under.
Staff very friendly, sites large with some slightly sloping, so bring and level chocks, timbers etc.
No complaints here and our 9 days were perfect, love the Bright district 10/10

Alpine shire should rip up their lease for the following reasons,  1/ staff not trained to handle powered sites 2/ management is Iill equipped to manage 3/ staff have no idea how to match sites to van size 4/ even after peak times garbage overflowing and right outside office - entry to park 5/ maintenance is virtually non existent
NRMA should br ashamed and the Alpine Shire for letting a organisation only interested in profit.
The only saving grace is the natural beauty of the trees and its location
Very clean amenities.   Staff was very helpful  and friendly.  Would recommend  this park.

Nice place. Stayed in a cabin.
But don't stay here when there are any holidays - manic!

Staying here off peak, the park was more than half empty so was able to have lots of space, and enjoy the flowing river. Pet friendly and the centre of town only 10 mins walk away. No sullage so we used our grey water tank, otherwise a bucket is necessary. Amenities basic, a bit dated but cleaned daily. Will be back.

The location of the park was excellent, close to shops and cafes. The site was very spacious, plenty of room for our car, marquue and tent. The pool was fantastic for my children!
We were disappointed by the cleanliness of the public amenities. The toilet was cleaned ever 24 hours. I think during peak periods it should be cleaned more than once. The hand soap had run out. The camp kitchen was quiet dirty following the Grand Final, so I took initiative to boil 2 kettles of water and wash the bench top of the camp kitchen.
We stayed three nights with our campertrailer. Good flat sites with handy amenities. Off season.

Initially favourable, although sites were very small. My 6 m campervan overlapped site behind me. However, a very loud, intoxicated and potentially violent group directly across from me and neighbouring friends ruined the experience. Camp management was not receptive when some of our group complained the next morning, saying they could not act on "word of mouth". One of the emergency phone numbers provided was inactive and late at night with a potentially dangerous situation none of us solo ladies felt like walking over to the office to look at the window for futher information. Group eventually settled at 11 pm but we decided to leave. Not a place I will visit again or recommend.

I am so glad I booked NRMA Bright Holiday Park...I had reservations due to the mixed reviews, but I was pleasantly impressed!! What a beautiful Park so close to town,with the creek running through, the drawbridge is gorgeous.The grounds were extremely well maintained and reception staff were happy, accommodating and so helpful with local eateries and places to visit. Big hats off to the new Managers!! We will be back.
Maryanne & David
Had my caravan. Reception are very friendly and are willing to help. Beautiful scenery, very clean amenities

Been to several NRMA’s in a caravan and thought there was a standard :/ unfortunately this one is below par in terms of up keep and the toilet block and showers were one of the worst I have encountered…..ever!!! Such a shame it’s in a lovely spot by the creek and v close to village
Clea, fresh, attractive cabins, lovely location, reasonable prices

Stayed in the River Cabin. The cabin was better than expected for a caravan park, when compared to other parks I've stayed at. It was clean, comfortable beds. Everything was as required. The friendly  staff are absolutely fantastic. Thank you to Admin staff,  Nicey  im sure i spelt that wrong my apologies. And one if the service/maintenance guys i chatted with, I didn't get a name (my bad). Was just a general chat but it was clear he was friendly and had great work ethic, eager to help in any way.
Great Park, perfect location.
Nice caravan park with a stream running through it

After camping throughout the VHC my partner and I decided to stay 1 night to break our trip on the way home to Sydney. The tiny patch of dirt we were given (even though there were many free and nicer sites around us) was horrible. We had local walkers passing right by our camp to a walkway and local kids playing in the creek right across from us, as well as private homes all around us. Maybe if I had paid less than $40 for the night I wouldn’t have cared as much, but the ridiculous price was horrendous for what we got. The ground wasn’t even flat! Maybe if the park had a bit more compassion for their guests they wouldn’t get so many subpar reviews.

Great park with excellent amenities and ambience. Wood fires allowed which was very much appreciated during the cold nights.
Laundry was good also. Have stayed here many times over the years and it has never disappointed. Thoroughly recommend.

We have been staying at the Bright NRMA caravan park for many years. Unfortunately over the last few years we have noticed a steady decline in the service and cleanliness of the park. This year, the toilets on our side of the park were filthy. I spoke to reception multiple times about the overflowing bin in the ladies toilets, along with the smeared faeces under the toilet bowl (which was still there after 4 days). Only 1 of 3 toilets had toilet paper. I could only find 1 working washing machine in two of the laundry areas. |Each time I spoke to somebody at reception I was told the issues would be rectified, but they never were.  After reading other reviews on this site, it is clear that this is a common occurrence of late. Considering the very high fees of unpowered sites and the unsanitary conditions, we will be staying elsewhere next year.

I can tell you the NRMA response to this review will again be denial of problems with this park, (formed this opinion their response  others reviews) rather than acknowledgement and perhaps a promise to remedy the obvious problems. |Booked powered site for my 8m+ van and friends 8m motor home. The sites were totally unacceptable, the MH could not be levelled or any where near level on the site. With my caravan I doubt the jockey wheel would remove van from the 4wd. I acknowledge the van park had limited sites available, we spent the next two hours going to sites said by staff to be suitable, they were not, simply they either did not have sullage or were still too steep and small to level up the MH.|We ended up getting a refund and moved on elsewhere, after wasting a food part of the day. |My observations of this park and our experience are as follows:|We chose NRMA park blindly believing because of ownership it would be good to excellent. |The roads are  rough and in need of repair in some areas. |The sites are not in the main suitable for larger rigs. |The sites are dusty and carpet filthy, yes you could clean them up with a blower vac, not the leaves just the dust and dirt. |Staff knew nothing about the various sites, new management and staff filling in. From grapevine around the sites constant change of management and staff is very common.|Head office staff allocating sites obviously know nothing about the actual sites other than perhaps measurement, and sullage, the slope is vey important with larger rigs. |FacilitiesI used whilst trying to find a site were absolutely dated and far from clean. |Site numbers are faded, poor and make location difficult. | Any way thank you NRMA for wasting a good part of my day, that would otherwise have been spent exploring Bright and spending some money in the local community. |In summary a park that needs a good planner, heaps of money spent on the park to update and correctly categorising it’s sites for suitable RVs. |Denial of problems again by NRMA are not acceptable, including that park management was very new, they cant fix problems here unless you assist and spend some money.

Beautiful setting.  Ideal location, walking distance to town.Magnificent trees. Morse's Creek runs through the middle of the park. Vans camped along the creek allow their grey water to run into the creek. There is minimal grey water sullage points and management don't do anything about the grey water issues. Grey water is allowed to run onto lower camp sites, across roads and into storm water points then into the creek. All facilities within the park are poorly maintained, not clean and run down. Many of the lights within the park don't work. Roads are eroded and full of pot holes. Only 2 washing machines working out of 6. Rubbish bins overflowing in some areas and stink.
Playground could do with some work, broken swing etc
Neat caravan park in a great location right on the edge of town.  Great facilities and helpful staff. Plenty of shady trees, ideal for summer and autumn camping.
Stayed in a Caravan,  great atmosphere,  friendly staff, good overall experience,  amenities were clean, great location,  will stay again.

What an amazing little park right at the edge of the Bright village ! Friendly staff that were very accommodating, ultra clean amenities, pool and spa. We had an unpowered site on creeks edge which was a great size for our vans. Definitely recommend for a summer stay.
Beautiful park and great location!

We have stayed at the park for many years and have always enjoyed it until this year. The amenities and run down, dirty, unfinished and in disrepair.  The fans wouldn't work in the camp kitchen and the doors would slide open due to the amount of dirt in the tracks. The floor hadn't been sweep or mopped in days.|There is no lighting at night time if walking back from the street.  The poleholes are the size of craters, our cabin sliding doors wouldn't slide or lock, the curtains were filthy and falling off the rail.  There was mould inside and outside of the shower and on the blinds, there's a large crack in the bathroom basin.  The fan switches don't have knobs to turn them on safely, the security light didn't work.  Management were extremely unpleasant and rude.  Great location but unfortunately this park has declined a lot in the last couple of years and extremely overpriced.

Have stayed at this park many times and it is brilliant but I think the best thing is the service. Nothing is an issue,  staff are incredibly helpful and seem to bend over backwards to give us poor old campers the best.
Big shout to MARIA this morning for taking extra time to sort the best possible site for our situation and constantly check if what she was providing suited us.
ABSOLUTELY FANTASTIC!!!!
Well played and thankyou Maria👍👍👍👍👍

Stayed there in 2023 for Easter. Went to rebook while there and was told had to wait for May 8th because we hadn't stayed two years consecutive. Rang May 8th and all booked out. How on earth you get in here regularly if you can't stay two years in a row. Two year rule is nonsensical. They keep saying it's difficult to get in, but we were in, and not allowed to keep coming back.

Great place for a holiday, but overcrowded during summer holidays. Apart from school holidays hard to beat for a restful stress free time away. Reasonable facilities, short walk to town centre with all the dining and fast food outlets. Walking, cycling paths in all directions swimming in summer etc. I am a fan.

Toilets not very clean and they always had a bad smell. From what l could tell only cleaned once a day. In busy period should be cleaned Twice a day.
Sites are too small for the size of vans. No parking space. If u are going to ask for size of van when booking make sure there is plenty of room to get in. Needs a major up grade in the park.
Really didn’t think the office staff were that friendly and never saw any staff walking around.

Excellent holiday park located close to town with beautiful Morses Creek running through it which is so much fun on hot days for swimming and tubing. Plenty of shade and clean facilities. Will definitely visit again.

We booked 4 campsites and 1 Cabin for 2 nights and paid around $1600approx which is not cheap but sadly one of the site was nothing but dirt and there was no one at reception to get it swapped over to some other site and they told us that there staff will leave at 3pm on 24th Dec and we got there 2:45pm and reception was already unattended any way we did self checkin.
Shared Bathrooms never got cleaned during our whole stay.
Laundry on site had both washers out of Order which wasn’t helpful either.
I found that it’s too expensive compared to the service they have offered us.

Loved this park. Spacious pitch, an easy walk into Bright. Toilets and showers well maintained and clean.
We will be back, but not in peak season. As all commercial parks do, they cram people in in the peak season which significantly detracts from the appeal.
We had a wonderful weekend here camping with our camper trailer.  Very dog friendly and really clean and toilets always clean.
2nd time here. Friendly and helpful staff..clean and tidy

Customer service is disappointing and maintenance is non existent. We have had a leak under our van for 12 months which has yet to be fixed. This has caused a waste of excessive water and other environment issues.
Awesome park very close to town.
Was visiting friends and was impressed with the layout and beautiful space.
Nice camping along the creek
Has amazing facilities has now been updated throughout the park. Can be busy. But was good to see the park doing a lot of maintenance through the quiet period to get ready for busy times

Great caravan park. We had a great site. Walking distance to shops and close to all the beautiful walking and cycling trails. Facilities were excellent. The only heated bathrooms I have come across and the camp kitchen was really well fitted out, clean and spacious . The sites were a great size. Able to take our own fire drum. Wood delivered to your site if requested.  Will definitely be back soon.
Caravan park on the creek, all the needs Cates for and a very good location.

Paid pretty good money for a river cabin only to find the cabin was quite run down and dirty inside when we arrived. They could definitely do with some makeovers for the price you pay. Will be looking elsewhere to stay on the river when we visit again.

Otherwise the park is clean and a good distance from town can walk in and out quite easily. Very overpriced for what you pay for. Could do with a tv in the bedroom but I did still enjoy it.
Paid $67 per night for unpowered site with one child. It's a nice park but the price is to high. Unfortunately I'll be looking elsewhere from now on.

We were pleasantly surprised at how good this park is. We are
not normally caravan park campers in our van but the unpowered sites are right on the river and plenty of room. Staff are friendly and so helpful, lovely family friendly park. We are in our late 50s and this absolutely ticked all our boxes. Great location, we will definitely be back. Well done to the staff at this park.

We stayed in a powered site with our caravan! The facilities are great. Staff are friendly and willing to help which is all you can ask for!
Location to town is great! Mini golf and playground for the kids!

Super-helpful and friendly reception staff who was able to assist when we had to reschedule our trip twice. Great location.
Clean rooms.

What an amazing spot, so close to town but you still feel you are out in nature.
Plenty of room and facilities were clean and tidy.
Fantastic customer service.
Had a fantastic stay and cannot wait to come back again

Great large park in the center of town. 6 minutes walk to the Brewery via the river walk. Lots of amenities like mini golf, swimming pool,  fire pit, covered BBQ areas, communal camp kitchen, and recreation room. Stay in a tent, caravan, cabin, or luxury safari tent.
The only waste dump point in town. Anyone can dump here $5 fee to dump or free for paying park guests.
Absolutely love staying here! awesome location, clean facilities and friendly staff
